description: Sector-specific screening controls for UK healthcare providers, regulated health and care activities, health data, clinical services, medical devices, and health-sector documentation.

metadata.scope: UK sector overlay controls for Health Sector Compliance Overlay. Intended to be used with uk_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official UK legal/regulatory sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.
metadata.territorial_scope_note: UK law can differ across England, Wales, Scotland, and Northern Ireland. This starter pack uses UK-wide sources where available and flags devolved or nation-specific application for human review.

primary_sources.name: CQC scope of registration / regulated activities
Regulating medical devices in the UK
UK GDPR and Data Protection Act 2018
NHS Data Security and Protection Toolkit

rules.title: CQC registration or regulated-activity evidence is present where applicable
rules.summary: Checks for CQC registration, regulated activities, registered manager, location, or provider registration evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Patient confidentiality and health-data privacy evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for patient confidentiality, medical records, health data, special-category data, or privacy controls.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Health data-security or DSP Toolkit evidence is present where applicable
rules.summary: Checks for DSP Toolkit, information governance, access controls, incident reporting, cyber hygiene, or NHS data-security evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: MHRA medical-device or medicines regulatory evidence is present where applicable
rules.summary: Checks for MHRA registration, UKCA marking, conformity assessment, post-market surveillance, vigilance, or medical device evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: No evidence of unauthorized patient-data disclosure is present
rules.summary: Screens for wording suggesting unauthorized disclosure or public exposure of identifiable patient data.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
